briefing.opening: Good morning/afternoon. We will now begin the climate briefing for April 20, 2026.

sections.title: 🇰🇷 Major Domestic Institutions Fully Commit to Carbon Neutrality During Climate Change Week
sections.content: Starting today, 'Climate Change Week' officially commences across the nation, with various campaigns and events actively underway to promote carbon neutrality in commemoration of the 56th Earth Day [1, 2, 4, 10]. In Yeosu, the simultaneous opening of 'International Green Transformation Week' and 'Climate Change Week' has accelerated green transition cooperation in the Jeonnam and Gwangju regions. The Ministry of Climate has also significantly expanded its Green Industry Alliance, forming the 'Republic of Korea Green Industry National Team' [1, 3, 12, 16]. Several local governments, including Gangwon Special Self-Governing Province, Chungcheongnam-do, and Jeonbuk Special Self-Governing Province, are operating carbon neutrality awareness and practice programs encompassing citizens and future generations. Companies such as KEPCO Nuclear Fuel and the Livestock Products Quality Assessment Institute have also declared their commitment to carbon neutrality both internally and externally [5, 6, 7, 11, 13, 14, 19, 27, 28, 29, 35, 36, 40, 43]. Notably, Chungcheongnam-do is demonstrating proactive steps, setting a carbon neutrality target for 2045, five years ahead of the government's goal, and reviewing the progress of its basic plan [26, 37, 42, 47, 48].

sections.title: 🌱 Accelerated Transition to Renewable Energy and Strengthened International Cooperation
sections.content: Renewable energy is strongly presented as a common solution to the climate crisis and energy security issues, accelerating the global transition to clean energy [20, 110]. Notably, in 2025, renewable energy set a 'first-ever' record, surpassing coal to become the world's largest source of electricity [111]. Domestically and internationally, India and the Republic of Korea have strengthened comprehensive economic cooperation in industry, trade, and carbon neutrality, signing four MOUs and issuing a joint statement on sustainability [31, 109]. Collaboration between companies like GS E&C and India's Suzlon Energy is also underway to target the Indian renewable energy market, and the government plans to support the installation of renewable energy generation facilities, such as solar power, in livestock farms and slaughterhouses [32, 50, 51]. Technological innovation is also prominent, with the introduction of solar and wind robot automation technology doubling installation speeds. Building methods that reduce energy consumption and generate their own power are gaining attention as solutions for carbon neutrality [45, 54, 56].

sections.title: ⚠️ Severe Impacts of Climate Change and Domestic/International Warnings
sections.content: The unpredictable impacts of climate change are becoming increasingly visible, with early 'uninvited guests' appearing [8]. Notably, wildfires that previously subsided at night are now observed to continue burning globally, a phenomenon likened to 'overtime' due to climate change [65, 75]. Domestically, a shocking report revealed that 12 Han River buses, promoted as 'eco-friendly' by Seoul City, emit 5,674 tons of greenhouse gases annually, equivalent to approximately 3,700 internal combustion engine vehicles [58, 59]. There are also direct threats to health, with research indicating that 'air environment stress' from fine dust, heatwaves, and wildfire smoke can severely harm kidney health [63, 97]. The international community warns that climate change is a 'slow-moving natural disaster,' reporting severe impacts on vulnerable populations and regions, such as parts of Israel becoming uninhabitable within decades and the education of 130 million African children being disrupted [81, 86, 88, 89, 90, 96, 106].

sections.title: 🌍 Expansion of Global Climate Action and Policy Discussions
sections.content: The international community is expanding discussions and actions to address climate change. A site inspection was conducted in Yeosu ahead of the 3rd Climate Week of the UNFCCC [24], and Türkiye announced its intention to pursue more global climate action as the COP31 presidency, aiming for a 'COP of Implementation' [83, 101]. At the UN Indigenous Peoples' Forum, war, climate change, and AI are being discussed as key agenda items [72, 107]. Local communities' adaptation efforts are also continuing, such as Kenyan women breaking fishing taboos in the face of threats to Lake Victoria due to climate change [64]. Domestically, a partisan debate is ongoing regarding the amendment of the Carbon Neutrality Act and the 61% reduction target for 2035. In the National Assembly, mandatory spatial planning and amendments to the Framework Act on Carbon Neutrality were discussed as solutions to renewable energy siting issues [46, 57]. At the Fossil Fuel Transition Summit held in Colombia, scientists urged action recommendations such as a 'halt to new fossil fuel expansion,' indicating a stronger global demand for climate action [112].

briefing.closing: Today's briefing highlights a day marked by both active domestic and international efforts to address climate change and warnings about the severe threats it poses. This concludes the climate briefing for April 20, 2026.
